projectDescription: Apache Doris is a high-performance, real-time MPP analytical database with columnar storage, vectorized query engine, and unified lakehouse capabilities.
keyFeatures: MPP-based vectorized query engine for sub-second analytical queries
Columnar storage with multiple index types (BloomFilter, Inverted, Sort Key)
Three data models: Duplicate Key, Unique Key (primary key upserts), and Aggregate Key
Real-time data ingestion via Stream Load, Routine Load (Kafka), and Binlog CDC
Unified Lakehouse: federated queries across Hive, Iceberg, Hudi, Paimon, Delta Lake
Materialized views (single-table auto-refresh and multi-table async)
MySQL-compatible protocol supporting standard SQL and BI tool integration
Horizontally scalable FE/BE architecture with high availability and multi-replica storage
Pipeline execution engine leveraging multi-core CPUs with CBO/RBO/HBO query optimizer
Storage-compute separation mode (Cloud) for elastic scaling
publicApiSurface: HTTP REST API (port 8030) — Stream Load, monitoring, schema queries
Arrow Flight SQL (port 8070) — high-throughput columnar data transfer
Routine Load: CREATE ROUTINE LOAD ... FROM KAFKA
Broker Load: LOAD LABEL ... WITH BROKER
INSERT INTO ... SELECT / INSERT INTO ... VALUES
CREATE CATALOG for external data source federation
CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW for pre-aggregated query acceleration
SHOW BACKENDS / SHOW FRONTENDS — cluster node management
